heading: Restriction on issuance of visas to nonimmigrants from countries that are state sponsors of international terrorism
label: 1735 Restriction on issuance of visas to nonimmigrants from countries that are state sponsors of international terrorism

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: No nonimmigrant visa under shall be issued to any alien from a country that is a state sponsor of international terrorism unless the Secretary of State determines, in consultation with the Attorney General and the heads of other appropriate United States agencies, that such alien does not pose a threat to the safety or national security of the United States. In making a determination under this subsection, the Secretary of State shall apply standards developed by the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Attorney General and the heads of other appropriate United States agencies, that are applicable to the nationals of such states.section 1101(a)(15) of this title

children.heading: State sponsor of international terrorism defined
children.label: b State sponsor of international terrorism defined

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In this section, the term “state sponsor of international terrorism” means any country the government of which has been determined by the Secretary of State under any of the laws specified in paragraph (2) to have repeatedly provided support for acts of international terrorism.

children.heading: Laws under which determinations were made
children.label: 2 Laws under which determinations were made
A
children.text: (or successor statute).Section 4605(j)(1)(A) of title 501See References in Text note below.1
